task_completion.description: Open the NOAA SST dataset in Panoply
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Panoply application window is open and the dataset 'sst.ltm.1991-2020.nc' is visible in the Sources or Datasets panel.

task_completion.description: Create a map plot for SST and set the time to February
task_completion.visual_evidence: A map plot window is open in Panoply displaying the 'sst' variable. The time/date control in the plot window (usually at the top or bottom) is set to February (e.g., Time Index 1 or a date in February).

task_completion.description: Export the North Atlantic regional map as a PNG
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save dialog in Panoply exporting an image, or a file manager/terminal showing 'north_atlantic_feb.png' successfully saved in the '/home/ga/Documents/GyreStudy' directory.

task_completion.description: Extract SST data at the four specified coordinates
task_completion.visual_evidence: The agent is viewing the 'Array' tab in Panoply, or using the map cursor/tooltip, to inspect the sea surface temperatures at the target coordinates: 30N 78W, 30N 12W, 45N 60W, and 45N 12W.

task_completion.description: Complete the thermal asymmetry report with extracted values and logical conclusions
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor is open showing 'thermal_asymmetry_report.txt' in '/home/ga/Documents/GyreStudy'. The file contains the recorded temperatures and explicitly states the conclusion: the western boundary is warmer at 30N, and the eastern boundary is warmer at 45N.

integrity.description: The agent used the Panoply GUI to view and extract data.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the Panoply graphical interface (menus, plot windows, data arrays) rather than the agent using terminal commands (like Python/xarray or ncdump) to bypass the requested tool.

integrity.description: The map was genuinely exported from Panoply.
integrity.visual_evidence: The exported 'north_atlantic_feb.png' should visually match the Panoply plot styling and be generated via Panoply's export function, not downloaded from the web or copied from elsewhere.

integrity.description: The recorded temperatures in the report are derived from the dataset.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values typed into the text report should match the expected ranges from the dataset (e.g., ~22.6C, ~18.0C, ~0.1C, ~12.5C), indicating genuine data extraction rather than hallucinated guesses.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Expected outputs: '/home/ga/Documents/GyreStudy/north_atlantic_feb.png' and '/home/ga/Documents/GyreStudy/thermal_asymmetry_report.txt'. Validated SST values for February: SUB_WEST (30N, 78W) is ~22.6°C [Valid: 19.0-24.0], SUB_EAST (30N, 12W) is ~18.0°C [Valid: 15.0-19.0], MID_WEST (45N, 60W) is ~0.1°C [Valid: -2.0-6.0], MID_EAST (45N, 12W) is ~12.5°C [Valid: 10.0-15.0]. Expected logic conclusion: Western boundary is warmer in the subtropics (30N), Eastern boundary is warmer in the mid-latitudes (45N).
